steps.title: Identify the sentence's structural need
steps.content: The sentence is: "She typically ___ them from sumac and cedar bark." The subject is "She" (singular) and "typically" signals a habitual action. The blank must be the main, conjugated verb — the only verb this clause has.

steps.title: Choose the correctly conjugated verb
steps.content: Rule: a main verb must be finite (conjugated) and agree with its subject. Non-finite forms (gerunds, infinitives) cannot stand alone as a predicate. A. "weaving" — present participle / gerund (non-finite). Fails: "She typically weaving them" has no finite verb; the sentence becomes a fragment. B. "to weave" — infinitive (non-finite). Fails: "She typically to weave them" is ungrammatical as a standalone predicate. C. "weaves" — third-person singular present tense (finite). Matches the singular subject "She" and correctly expresses a habitual action with "typically." Correct. D. "to be weaving" — progressive infinitive (non-finite). Fails: same problem as B; an infinitive cannot serve as the main verb. That matches choice C — C is correct.
